key_insight: Unsupervised domain adaptation using style transfer plus a Swin Transformer V2 improved mammography calcification classification across different sites and imaging vendors.
clinical_relevance: Better cross-site generalization could help AI mammography tools perform more reliably on new hospitals and imaging systems.
method: Generate vendor- and technique-specific training images with AdaIN and CycleGAN
Train a supervised classifier with Swin Transformer V2 as the backbone
Test generalization with cross-validation and external validation across multi-site datasets
evidence_signal: The framework was evaluated on OPTIMAM (n=2994), with external validation on EMBED (n=125) and Duke Calcification Dataset v1 (n=788), improving AUC from 0.68 to 0.72 on EMBED and from 0.68 to 0.73 on Duke.
watch_item: Performance gains are promising but still modest, so broader validation and careful assessment of dataset shift and image quality are needed before real-world deployment.
paper_title: Unsupervised Domain Adaptation for Calcification Classification in Mammography Across Multi-Site Datasets
